## mttr_history

The append-only companion to [`mttr_current`](#mttr_current): same source,
columns, and provenance, but instead of holding only the latest state it
accumulates a dated snapshot of every incident on each upload.

Each row is one incident (`incident_id`) as it appeared on one snapshot `date`,
so an incident's metrics can change from one snapshot to the next as it is
re-triaged. A row is unique on (`incident_id`, `date`).

`Keywords`: MTTR, MTTA, MTTM, incident, incident response, snapshot, history, severity, reliability, SRE, mean time to resolve

### Fields

Every column is identical to [`mttr_current`](#mttr_current), with one addition:

| Field Name | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| date | string | Snapshot date as `YYYY-MM-DD` text; the grain that lets this table accumulate. Equals the date portion of `fetched_at` |
